preamble.summary: Targum Jonathan on Jeremiah navigates the tension between divine judgment and covenant faithfulness. The new covenant prophecy (31:31-34) is rendered with striking literalism. Anti-anthropomorphism governs descriptions of God's emotional response to Israel's unfaithfulness (anger, grief, jealousy). The Messianic 'Branch' passages (23:5, 33:15) receive explicit identification. The Memra functions as the agent of both judgment and future restoration.
preamble.notable_renderings: The call narrative (ch. 1) uses standard anti-anthropomorphic language. The new covenant (31:31-34) is remarkably literal. The Messianic Branch (23:5) is rendered as the righteous Messiah. The Temple sermons use Shekinah language. The confessions of Jeremiah receive minimal theological adjustment, preserving the prophet's raw emotion.
preamble.theological_themes: The Memra as agent of prophetic commissioning and covenant renewal; Shekinah departure and return as the theological framework for exile and restoration; Messianic Branch theology; the new covenant as Torah internalization; anti-anthropomorphism in divine pathos.

renderings.hebrew_text: בְּטֶרֶם אֶצָּרְךָ בַבֶּטֶן יְדַעְתִּיךָ
renderings.targum_text: ad la tzayartakh bemeiayya yeda'atakh
renderings.targum_rendering: Before I formed you in the womb I knew you.
renderings.mt_rendering: Before I formed you in the womb I knew you.
notes: Jeremiah's calling is rendered literally. Divine foreknowledge is not an anthropomorphism requiring adjustment — it is an attribute of God's eternal knowledge.

renderings.hebrew_text: וַיִּשְׁלַח יְהוָה אֶת־יָדוֹ וַיַּגַּע עַל־פִּי
renderings.targum_text: ushedar Adonai yat pitgam nvu'ateih visamkha al pumi
renderings.targum_rendering: And the LORD sent forth the word of his prophecy and placed it upon my mouth.
renderings.mt_rendering: Then the LORD put out his hand and touched my mouth.
notes: God's hand reaching out and touching Jeremiah's mouth becomes 'the word of his prophecy' being placed upon Jeremiah's mouth. The physical gesture is replaced with verbal prophetic commissioning — the Word, not the hand, empowers the prophet.

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּי אִתְּךָ אֲנִי נְאֻם יְהוָה לְהַצִּילֶךָ
renderings.targum_text: arei Memri besiyyakhah amar Adonai leshezavutakh
renderings.targum_rendering: For my Memra is in your help, says the LORD, to deliver you.
renderings.mt_rendering: For I am with you, declares the LORD, to deliver you.
notes: The promise of divine presence to the prophet follows the standard Memra formula. Jeremiah's prophetic ministry is sustained by the Memra's accompaniment.

renderings.hebrew_text: בָּעֵת הַהִיא יִקְרְאוּ לִירוּשָׁלַיִם כִּסֵּא יְהוָה
renderings.targum_text: be'iddana hahi yitqerun liYerushalem kurseih diyeqara daAdonai
renderings.targum_rendering: At that time they shall call Jerusalem the throne of the glory of the LORD.
renderings.mt_rendering: At that time Jerusalem shall be called the throne of the LORD.
notes: Jerusalem is not the LORD's throne directly but the throne of his glory (yeqar). The eschatological transformation of Jerusalem is mediated through glory-theology.

renderings.hebrew_text: הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵמָּה
renderings.targum_text: heikhela daAdonai heikhela daAdonai heikhela daAdonai innun
renderings.targum_rendering: The Temple of the LORD, the Temple of the LORD, the Temple of the LORD are these.
renderings.mt_rendering: The temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD are these.
notes: The deceptive refrain is rendered literally — Jonathan preserves the irony of false confidence in the Temple's inviolability.

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּי לְכוּ נָא אֶל מְקוֹמִי אֲשֶׁר בְּשִׁילוֹ אֲשֶׁר שִׁכַּנְתִּי שְׁמִי שָׁם
renderings.targum_text: arei izelu ke'an le'atri diveShiloh di ashrei Shekhineti tamman
renderings.targum_rendering: For go now to my place which was in Shiloh, where I caused my Shekinah to dwell there.
renderings.mt_rendering: Go now to my place that was in Shiloh, where I made my name dwell at first.
notes: God's 'name dwelling' at Shiloh is rendered as 'Shekinah dwelling' at Shiloh. The destruction of Shiloh is a precedent for Shekinah departure — the Shekinah can leave a sanctuary it once inhabited.

renderings.hebrew_text: וַיהוָה אֱלֹהִים אֱמֶת הוּא אֱלֹהִים חַיִּים וּמֶלֶךְ עוֹלָם
renderings.targum_text: vaAdonai Elohim hu Elah qeshot hu Elah chayyin umalka de'alma
renderings.targum_rendering: But the LORD God — he is the true God, he is the living God and the eternal King.
renderings.mt_rendering: But the LORD is the true God; he is the living God and the everlasting King.
notes: The confession of God's uniqueness amid idol polemic is rendered literally. Attributes like 'true,' 'living,' and 'eternal king' are non-anthropomorphic and require no adjustment.

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּסֵּא כָבוֹד מָרוֹם מֵרִאשׁוֹן מְקוֹם מִקְדָּשֵׁנוּ
renderings.targum_text: kursei yeqara de'illaya min qadmai atar beit maqdashana
renderings.targum_rendering: The throne of glory on high from the beginning is the place of our Temple.
renderings.mt_rendering: A glorious throne set on high from the beginning is the place of our sanctuary.
notes: The heavenly throne of glory corresponds to the earthly Temple. Jonathan establishes the two-tiered cosmology: the heavenly throne and the earthly sanctuary mirror each other.

renderings.hebrew_text: הִנֵּה יָמִים בָּאִים נְאֻם יְהוָה וַהֲקִמֹתִי לְדָוִד צֶמַח צַדִּיק
renderings.targum_text: ha yomayya atyin amar Adonai va'aqim leDavid Meshicha zakkaya
renderings.targum_rendering: Behold, the days are coming, says the LORD, when I will raise up for David a righteous Messiah.
renderings.mt_rendering: Behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will raise up for David a righteous Branch.
notes: The 'righteous Branch' (tsemach tsaddiq) is rendered 'righteous Messiah' (Meshicha zakkaya). Jonathan explicitly decodes the botanical metaphor as Messianic prophecy, consistent with his treatment of Isaiah 11:1. The Branch is the Messiah.

renderings.hebrew_text: וְזֶה שְּׁמוֹ אֲשֶׁר יִקְרְאוֹ יְהוָה צִדְקֵנוּ
renderings.targum_text: veden shemeih di yiqreinnei Adonai tzidqana
renderings.targum_rendering: And this is the name by which he shall be called: the LORD is our righteousness.
renderings.mt_rendering: And this is the name by which he will be called: 'The LORD is our righteousness.'
notes: The Messiah's throne name 'YHWH-Tsidqenu' is preserved literally. Jonathan does not shy away from a name that applies YHWH to the Messianic king, though it can be read as a statement about God rather than an identification of the Messiah with God.

renderings.hebrew_text: יְהוָה מִמָּרוֹם יִשְׁאָג
renderings.targum_text: Adonai min merom beit Shekhinteih yigbirah qaleih
renderings.targum_rendering: The LORD from the height of the house of his Shekinah shall raise his voice.
renderings.mt_rendering: The LORD will roar from on high.
notes: 'On high' becomes 'the height of the house of his Shekinah,' localizing the heavenly source of judgment in Shekinah cosmology. God does not 'roar' (an animal metaphor) but 'raises his voice.'

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּי אָנֹכִי יָדַעְתִּי אֶת־הַמַּחֲשָׁבֹת אֲשֶׁר אָנֹכִי חֹשֵׁב עֲלֵיכֶם... מַחְשְׁבוֹת שָׁלוֹם
renderings.targum_text: arei ana yeda'na yat chushebanayya de'ana chashiv aleikhon... chushebanei shelam
renderings.targum_rendering: For I know the thoughts which I think concerning you... thoughts of peace.
renderings.mt_rendering: For I know the plans I have for you... plans for welfare.
notes: God's knowledge of his own plans is rendered literally. Divine planning is an attribute of wisdom, not an anthropomorphism. The promise of peace/welfare is directed to the exiles.

renderings.hebrew_text: וְעָבְדוּ אֵת יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיהֶם וְאֵת דָּוִד מַלְכָּם אֲשֶׁר אָקִים לָהֶם
renderings.targum_text: viyiphlchun qodam Adonai Elahehon viyishttam'un leMeshicha bar David malkhon di aqim lehon
renderings.targum_rendering: And they shall serve before the LORD their God, and they shall obey the Messiah son of David, their king, whom I will raise up for them.
renderings.mt_rendering: And they shall serve the LORD their God and David their king, whom I will raise up for them.
notes: 'David their king' is interpreted as 'the Messiah son of David, their king.' Jonathan makes the Messianic identification explicit: the future David is not the historical David resurrected but the Messiah who descends from David's line.

renderings.hebrew_text: וְהָיָה אַדִּירוֹ מִמֶּנּוּ וּמֹשְׁלוֹ מִקִּרְבּוֹ יֵצֵא
renderings.targum_text: vihavei rabrehon minnehon uMeshikhon mibeinehon yitgeli
renderings.targum_rendering: And their great one shall be from among them, and their Messiah shall be revealed from their midst.
renderings.mt_rendering: Their prince shall be from themselves; their ruler shall come from their midst.
notes: The ruler 'coming from their midst' becomes the Messiah 'revealed from their midst' (yitgeli). The Messiah does not merely appear — he is revealed, using the same theology of divine disclosure applied throughout Jonathan.

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּי הָיִיתִי לְיִשְׂרָאֵל לְאָב
renderings.targum_text: arei Memri havat leYisrael le'abba
renderings.targum_rendering: For my Memra has been a father to Israel.
renderings.mt_rendering: For I am a father to Israel.
notes: God's fatherhood of Israel is mediated through the Memra. Even the most intimate relational metaphor is expressed through the Word, maintaining the Memra as the mode of all divine-human relationship.

renderings.hebrew_text: הִנֵּה יָמִים בָּאִים נְאֻם יְהוָה וְכָרַתִּי אֶת בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל... בְּרִית חֲדָשָׁה
renderings.targum_text: ha yomayya atyin amar Adonai ve'agzar im beit Yisrael... keyam chadatt
renderings.targum_rendering: Behold, the days are coming, says the LORD, when I will make with the house of Israel... a new covenant.
renderings.mt_rendering: Behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel.
notes: The new covenant prophecy is rendered with striking literalism. Jonathan does not qualify, restrict, or reinterpret 'new covenant' (keyam chadatt). This is the verse cited in Hebrews 8:8-12 as fulfilled in Christ. The targum's literalism here is remarkable given its tendency to interpret elsewhere.

renderings.hebrew_text: נָתַתִּי אֶת תּוֹרָתִי בְּקִרְבָּם וְעַל לִבָּם אֶכְתְּבֶנָּה
renderings.targum_text: eittein yat oraiti begevvehon ve'al libbehon ikhtevinnah
renderings.targum_rendering: I will put my Torah in their inward parts, and upon their heart I will write it.
renderings.mt_rendering: I will put my law within them, and I will write it on their hearts.
notes: The Torah internalization is rendered literally. The new covenant does not replace Torah but internalizes it. Jonathan's treatment suggests the new covenant is Torah-centered renewal, not Torah abolition.

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּי אֶסְלַח לַעֲוֺנָם וּלְחַטָּאתָם לֹא אֶזְכָּר עוֹד
renderings.targum_text: arei ashbiq lechovehon ulechotehon la edkar tub
renderings.targum_rendering: For I will forgive their iniquity, and their sin I will remember no more.
renderings.mt_rendering: For I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more.
notes: The climax of the new covenant: divine forgiveness and the end of sin-remembrance. Jonathan renders this literally, preserving the unconditional promise of forgiveness that defines the new covenant.

renderings.hebrew_text: בַּיָּמִים הָהֵם וּבָעֵת הַהִיא אַצְמִיחַ לְדָוִד צֶמַח צְדָקָה
renderings.targum_text: beyomayya ha'innun uve'iddana hahi atzammach leDavid Meshicha detzidqa
renderings.targum_rendering: In those days and at that time I will cause to sprout for David a Messiah of righteousness.
renderings.mt_rendering: In those days and at that time I will cause a righteous Branch to spring up for David.
notes: The second Branch prophecy in Jeremiah is also rendered as 'Messiah of righteousness' (Meshicha detzidqa). The agricultural metaphor of sprouting is preserved alongside the Messianic identification.

renderings.hebrew_text: וְזֶה אֲשֶׁר יִקְרָא לָהּ יְהוָה צִדְקֵנוּ
renderings.targum_text: veden di yitqerun lah min qodam Adonai bidqayin yitaseyan lana
renderings.targum_rendering: And this is the name by which she [Jerusalem] will be called: 'Before the LORD our righteousness will be accomplished for us.'
renderings.mt_rendering: And this is the name by which it will be called: 'The LORD is our righteousness.'
notes: While 23:6 applied the name to the Messianic king, 33:16 applies it to Jerusalem. Jonathan renders the city's name as a theological statement: before the LORD, righteousness is accomplished. The name YHWH-Tsidqenu has both personal (Messianic) and civic (Jerusalem) applications.

renderings.hebrew_text: כִּי אִתְּךָ אָנִי נְאֻם יְהוָה לְהוֹשִׁעֶךָ
renderings.targum_text: arei Memri besiyyakhah amar Adonai leshezavutakh
renderings.targum_rendering: For my Memra is in your help, says the LORD, to save you.
renderings.mt_rendering: For I am with you, declares the LORD, to save you.
notes: The concluding promise to Jacob/Israel uses the standard Memra-help formula. Even amid oracles against the nations, God's relationship with Israel is Memra-mediated.
